title: Replace naive CV with grouped, stratified, time-aware splits plus embargoed encodings
summary: This dataset is repeated race sequences, so row-random CV can overstate AUC by leaking race-, stint-, and season-specific patterns.
rationale: This dataset is repeated race sequences, so row-random CV can overstate AUC by leaking race-, stint-, and season-specific patterns. scikit-learn documents both non-overlapping grouped folds and time-aware splits with a gap to avoid future-to-past contamination.
implementation_hint: Evaluate `StratifiedGroupKFold` on `Race_Year` or `Race_Year` x `Driver`, then a stricter forward split ordered by race date/year with a 1-2 lap embargo inside each sequence for any lag/target encoding features. Recompute all count/mean encodings OOF only.
expected_effect: May lower local CV but improve leaderboard transfer and help identify which ideas are genuinely robust.
risk: The stricter scheme can be noisier with only 26 races; if test distribution mixes years differently, over-conservative CV may underfit tuning.
